behaviors.description: TSP heuristic produces a route ≤ 1.5x optimal for inputs up to 20 stops
behaviors.justification: The approximation guarantee is a pure algorithmic property. A mock that returns a pre-set route length would hide the real heuristic's behavior. The test needs to verify the actual algorithm against known-optimal solutions (computable via dynamic programming for small N=20), which requires only synthetic coordinates and no I/O.
behaviors.test_description: Create synthetic coordinate sets in known patterns (grid, circle, random). For each, compute true optimal distance via TSP-DP algorithm. Run the heuristic. Verify actual_distance / optimal_distance ≤ 1.5 across test cases with N=5, 10, 15, 20 stops. Test multiple graph topologies to ensure the guarantee holds.

behaviors.description: Time estimates computed correctly as distance/speed from configured road-type speed
behaviors.justification: The formula (time = distance / speed) is simple, but the real failure is incorrect speed lookup by road type. A pure mock that returns the correct speed hides bugs in configuration loading or the road-type → speed mapping. Must test the full chain: load config → look up speed → compute time, not just the arithmetic.
behaviors.test_description: Set up test road-type config (e.g., 'highway'→60mph, 'residential'→25mph, 'arterial'→45mph). Create route segments with known distances and road types. Call the time-window computation. Verify time = distance / configured_speed for each road type. Test at least 3 road types, including edge cases like unknown types.

behaviors.description: Shift-hours validator rejects route exceeding remaining hours, returns excess amount
behaviors.justification: Validation logic (sum segment times, compare to remaining hours, calculate excess) is deterministic arithmetic. No external dependencies or I/O. Test can pass in pre-computed segment times and remaining hours directly. Pure function with no mocks needed.
behaviors.test_description: Test three cases: (1) Route 8h fits in 10h remaining → valid=true, excess=null. (2) Route 12h exceeds 10h remaining → valid=false, excess=2h. (3) Route exactly at limit (10h, 10h remaining) → valid=true. Verify excess is a precise numeric difference, edge case of 1 minute over threshold, and null vs zero distinction.

behaviors.description: Service skips address when geocoding fails (no results), optimizes remaining stops, reports which were skipped
behaviors.justification: Geocoding is an external system boundary. A pure mock that always succeeds never triggers the skip logic; the real failure mode is 'API returns no results for this address.' Must test the service's graceful degradation at the geocoding boundary, simulating mixed responses (some resolve, some fail).
behaviors.test_description: Mock the geocoding API to resolve 4 of 5 input addresses successfully and return 'no results' for 1. Call service with all 5 addresses. Verify: (1) geocoding called for all 5, (2) 4 coordinates obtained, (3) 1 marked as skipped, (4) route optimization runs on 4 stops only, (5) skipped address clearly identified in output report with reason.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses consolidated into single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice
behaviors.justification: Duplicate detection and consolidation is pure data transformation logic before geocoding. Test can provide synthetic addresses and verify consolidation output without mocking geocoding or any external I/O. The logic is deterministic string comparison and item merging.
behaviors.test_description: Input list: [('Main St', items_batch_1), ('Oak Ave', items_batch_2), ('Main St', items_batch_3)]. Call consolidation. Verify output: two unique stops—'Main St' with items merged from both batches, 'Oak Ave' unchanged. Route optimization then proceeds on 2 stops. Test edge cases: whitespace variations, case sensitivity, multiple duplicates of same address.
